whatItDoes: Converts lists between multiple formats: one-per-line, CSV, numbered, bulleted, JSON array, and comma-separated. Select the source format and target format, and the list is instantly converted.
whyItExists: Lists come in many formats across different tools and contexts. Manually reformatting a list from one style to another is tedious and error-prone, especially for long lists.
whoShouldUse: Developers migrating data between formats, content creators reformatting lists for different platforms, and anyone who regularly works with structured lists.
useCases: Convert a newline-separated list from a spreadsheet into a JSON array for code
Turn a numbered markdown list into a simple one-per-line format
Transform comma-separated values into a bulleted markdown list
Convert a JSON array into CSV format for import into a spreadsheet
instructions: Select the source format of your input list using the From dropdown
Select the desired output format using the To dropdown
Paste or type your list into the input area
The converted list appears instantly in the output area
Click Copy to copy the result to your clipboard
examples: CSV (apple, banana, cherry) → Numbered: 1. apple\n2. banana\n3. cherry
JSON array ["a", "b", "c"] → Bulleted: - a\n- b\n- c
bestPractices: Make sure the From format matches your actual input format for correct parsing
For JSON array input, each element should be a simple string
Trailing commas in CSV input are handled automatically
commonMistakes: Selecting the wrong source format, which causes items to be parsed incorrectly
Expecting nested JSON arrays to be flattened — only simple arrays are supported
Forgetting to trim extra whitespace around items in CSV input
faq: Can I convert from JSON to CSV? — Yes, select JSON Array as From and CSV as To.
Does it handle nested lists? — No, only flat lists of simple strings are supported.
What happens with empty lines? — Empty lines are automatically filtered out from the output.
